rationale_for_baseline_tier: Painful acute contact dermatitis needing prompt assessment and prescription, but with intact epidermis and no airway/systemic involvement — urgent primary/urgent care (T3) with a clear emergency airway safety-net.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling of the face/lips/tongue/throat, trouble breathing or swallowing, or throat tightness/voice change
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Use adrenaline auto-injector if available and call 000 immediately
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your face, lips, tongue or throat start to swell, or you have any trouble breathing or swallowing — use an adrenaline pen if you have one and call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Thick foul-smelling yellow pus, spreading warmth/redness, or fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests secondary bacterial infection needing antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the weeping turns to thick smelly yellow pus, spreads, or you get a fever, get urgent review.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the patient can call for help and has a low threshold to attend ED if any swelling develops overnight.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a strong skin reaction to the product. Rinse well, apply the steroid lotion thinly twice daily, take the antihistamine, and throw the product away. Watch closely for any facial or throat swelling.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/urgent care; call 000 for airway/breathing symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the skin; 000 immediately for any facial/throat swelling or breathing difficulty.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note acute contact dermatitis from an unregulated topical product, treatment started, and request review + safe androgenetic alopecia options.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Simple self-care advice with no airway/anaphylaxis safety-net after a chemical exposure.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Evolving airway angioedema is missed and the patient obstructs before seeking help.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays potent topical steroid and leaves the reaction to worsen.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency-ambulance/burns-unit escalation for an uncomplicated contact dermatitis with no airway involvement.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot assess burn depth or the airway remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as contact dermatitis with decontamination and topical steroid, and give an explicit airway/anaphylaxis safety-net with a low threshold to attend ED.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-escalating to a burns unit, or reassuring without any airway safety-net.
